commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; this protected maintainer PR needs human acceptance of the fail-closed auth compatibility change and final CI.; Cleared: The diff narrows the WhatsApp credential trust boundary using existing fs-safe SDK helpers and does not add new dependencies, workflows, secret exposure, or third-party code execution. (sha=86f1bb8c38c7fcc785ae40e02cdbf448e1edeab0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds WhatsApp credential-file helpers, routes auth reads, setup detection, socket startup, backup restore/rotation, and atomic saves through symlink-rejecting regular-file checks, and adds focused symlink regression coverage plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main uses stat/readFile/copyFile and reaches Baileys auth state without a symlink preflight, while the PR adds symlink fixture tests for those paths. I did not execute the filesystem repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; this protected maintainer PR needs human acceptance of the fail-closed auth compatibility change and final CI.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): reject symlinked credential files
target.body:
## Summary

This hardens WhatsApp Web credential handling around the `creds.json` trust boundary. Previously, several auth paths used ordinary `stat`/`readFile`/`copyFile` checks, which can follow symlinks and make a symlinked credential path look like a valid WhatsApp session. This PR centralizes WhatsApp credential file access so `creds.json` and `creds.json.bak` are only accepted as regular files reached through non-symlinked credential-directory parents.

Reviewer path:

- `extensions/whatsapp/src/creds-files.ts` defines the new credential-file contract: parent path validation, regular-file-or-missing assertions, safe raw reads, safe stat helpers, and separate "regular file exists" vs. "valid linked creds" checks.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.ts` routes auth existence, auth snapshots, self-id reads, age checks, and backup restore through those helpers.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/session.ts` rejects unsafe credential paths before creating the Baileys auth state, rechecks after pending save-queue drain, and rotates backups with guarded atomic writes instead of `copyFileSync`.
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/creds-persistence.ts` adds a shared raw atomic writer that validates the target before writing and again in `beforeRename`, so a symlink swap at the final write boundary is rejected.

## Behavior Change

Unsafe WhatsApp Web credential paths now fail closed:

- Symlinked `creds.json` is treated as missing for read-only auth checks.
- Symlinked `creds.json` blocks socket startup before Baileys reads auth state.
- Symlinked auth directories or symlinked credential-directory parents block startup before creating directories or reading credentials.
- Symlinked backup files are not used for restore.
- Backup rotation does not write through a symlinked `creds.json.bak`.
- Atomic credential saves reject unsafe primary credential paths before and immediately before rename.

Existing recovery behavior is preserved where it matters:

- Missing credentials still report as not linked.
- Truncated regular credentials can still be restored from a valid regular backup.
- Valid large regular credentials are no longer mistaken for corrupt credentials.
- Legacy default-account detection still selects a truncated regular legacy `creds.json` so backup recovery can run, but ignores symlinked legacy credentials.

## Why This Shape

The fix keeps the ownership boundary inside the WhatsApp plugin instead of adding generic policy to core. The plugin owns the WhatsApp credential layout, so the new helpers describe exactly what this plugin considers a safe Web credential file and make every caller use that same contract.

Using `openclaw/plugin-sdk/security-runtime` keeps the implementation on existing fs-safe primitives:

- `assertNoSymlinkParentsSync` for credential-directory path validation.
- `statRegularFileSync`, `readRegularFile`, and `readRegularFileSync` for no-symlink regular-file reads/stats.
- `replaceFileAtomic` with `beforeRename` for the final save/restore/backup write boundary.

## Tests

Focused coverage was added for:

- read-only auth helpers ignoring symlinked `creds.json`
- linked-state and self-id readers treating symlinked creds as missing
- socket startup rejecting symlinked creds, symlinked auth dirs, and symlinked parent dirs before Baileys reads
- atomic credential saves rejecting symlinked primary credential paths
- backup restore refusing symlinked source backups and symlinked primary targets
- backup rotation refusing symlinked backup targets
- preserving truncated regular creds recovery and valid large regular creds

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.whatsapp-auth.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.setup.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/session.test.ts`
- PR CI restarted after rebase on head `86f1bb8c38`

## Live Tests

Additional live validation was run on a local Linux OpenClaw installation at head `86f1bb8c38c7fcc785ae40e02cdbf448e1edeab0` using isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` / `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18815`, and `pnpm gateway:watch --verbose`; the existing local gateway service was stopped first and restored afterward.

- Symlinked `creds.json` status: A valid-looking `creds.json` was placed behind a file symlink.
  Status ignored it and reported WhatsApp as `not-linked`, `linked: false`, and `running: false`.
- Symlinked auth-dir parent status: The configured auth dir lived under a symlinked parent directory.
  Status did not follow the parent symlink and kept the account `not-linked` and stopped.
- Symlinked `creds.json` login: Login was run with `creds.json` as a symlink to an external target.
  It failed before QR rendering with the unsafe credential-path error and left the target file unchanged.
- Direct symlinked `authDir` status: The configured WhatsApp account `authDir` itself was a symlink to a real credential directory.
  Status ignored the credentials behind that symlink and kept the account `not-linked` and stopped.
- Direct symlinked `authDir` login: Login was run against an account whose configured `authDir` was a symlink.
  It failed before QR rendering with the unsafe credential-path error and did not modify the target credentials.
- Backup and atomic-write tests: Focused Vitest covered backup restore, backup rotation, and credential save boundaries.
  It proved symlinked backups/targets are rejected while regular backup restore still works.

Additional command run after live validation: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/session.test.ts` (`2 passed`, `42 passed`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp Web credential reads and writes now reject direct symlinked `creds.json` files, symlinked backup credential paths, and symlinked auth directory parents instead of reading or writing through them.
Real environment tested: Local Linux Codex worktree with filesystem symlink fixtures in focused Vitest coverage.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/auth-store.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.whatsapp-auth.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.setup.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/session.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Tests cover symlinked primary creds before reads and writes, symlinked backup creds before restore and backup rotation, symlinked auth directories, symlinked auth parents before directory creation, truncated-creds backup selection, and valid large creds.
Observed result after fix: Focused WhatsApp credential/auth/session tests passed after the rebase; PR CI restarted for head `86f1bb8c38`.
target.author: mcaxtr
